tldr: Move from Anteon's K8s-centric observability to Sensu Go, an event-driven monitoring pipeline for hybrid and multi-cloud infrastructure.
why_switch: MIT-licensed open-source core with a freemium tier.
Event-driven model fits hybrid cloud + on-prem better than pure K8s tools.
Sumo Logic backing for enterprise support if needed.
export_method: Anteon does not document a unified bundle export. Pull configuration via its API or self-hosted DB; archive historical data to object storage if needed. See Anteon's official docs for current export endpoints.
import_method: Sensu Go is installed by deploying the backend, agents on each host/cluster, and the sensuctl CLI (see sensu.io docs for current install steps). Define checks, handlers, and pipelines as YAML/JSON resources and apply via sensuctl. Wire notifications via handlers (Slack, PagerDuty, email). For Kubernetes, install Sensu agents as a DaemonSet and define checks against pods/nodes.

concept_mapping.note: Sensu agents run anywhere — K8s, VMs, on-prem.
Event-driven model: checks generate events, handlers act on them.
Rebuild; integrate metrics via Sensu's metric pipelines.
Sensu is monitoring/events-focused; pair with ELK/Loki for logs.
Confirm tier features in Sensu's pricing docs.

gotchas: Sensu's event/pipeline model differs from Prometheus-style polling — expect a learning curve.
Sensu Go does not cover logs or traces; build a complementary stack.
Releases are less frequent than active SaaS competitors; plan upgrade cadence accordingly.
Configuration complexity grows with pipeline count — keep it in Git.
Confirm commercial tier scope with Sumo Logic / Sensu sales if you need support.
Historical Anteon data won't replay into Sensu; restart with a fresh baseline.

email.subject: Migrating from Anteon to Sensu Go
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing to switch from Anteon to Sensu Go for monitoring across our hybrid cloud + on-prem footprint. Sensu Go is MIT-licensed, event-driven, and fits non-Kubernetes hosts better than our current setup.

Work: deploy Sensu backend; roll out agents (DaemonSet on K8s, packages on VMs); translate existing alerts into Sensu checks and pipelines; wire Slack/PagerDuty handlers.

Note: Sensu doesn't cover logs/traces — we'll keep or add a separate stack for those.

Estimated effort: 16-40 hours plus a parallel-run window.

Will share a detailed migration doc.

Thanks.
